page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, showing strong throughput in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression results. Large-model LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with the 7B model achieving only 5.7 tokens/sec.

Given its high memory density and bundled high-speed NVMe storage, the r5dn.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads that require high memory-to-CPU ratios and rapid local I/O. The combination of 100 Gbps network bandwidth and strong memory bandwidth makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, Redis in-memory caching, high-throughput static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of bundled NVMe SSD local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput Redis operations, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are weaker. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, network-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and in-memory caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring 768 GB RAM, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processors
768 GB of DDR4 memory paired with 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale web serving
